lines.text: Welcome back. The clock reads 23:00. We are four hours in.
[pause] I'm Sister Marigold. For the next hour, we're going to breathe together.
Find a comfortable position. Seated or lying down. The choice is yours.
Breathe in through your nose. [pause] Count to four as you hold.
Release through your mouth. Slow. Intentional. [pause] Again.
In. Hold. Release. In. Hold. Release.
[pause] Do you notice the quality of the air in your space? The air you chose to surround yourself with tonight?
That air is yours. It belongs to you because you decided it would.
Breathe in. [pause] The ones outside are not breathing this air.
They made a different choice. [pause] That is not your responsibility.
Scan your body. Start at the crown of your head. Any tension there?
Release it. Let it drain down through your shoulders.
Your neck. Your chest. Notice where you're holding fear.
The fear is information. [pause] Acknowledge it. Don't fight it.
Your hands. Are they clenched? Open them. Feel the weight of your fingers against whatever surface supports you.
[pause] You are still here. That matters.
Breathing in. Breathing out. The night continues without you having to do anything but this.
The barricades hold themselves. The locks hold themselves. You only have to breathe.
[pause] I want you to think about a person you decided not to forgive. Don't say the name.
Just hold them in your mind. The shape of their absence from your life.
Tonight, they are somewhere in this city. Or they are not. [pause] That is no longer your decision.
You decided long ago. You are allowed to stop deciding it over and over.
Exhale that person. Let them go. [pause] You don't need them to survive this night.
Continue breathing. In. [pause] Hold. [pause] Out.
The signal tonight is strange. You may have noticed the cuts in the broadcast.
Static is part of the meditation. Interruption is part of the peace.
Nothing is permanent. Not even this hour. [pause] Not even this night.
Breathe in the knowledge that you will outlast the Purge.
Eight hours remain. [pause] You have already survived a third of your waiting.
Your body knows how to wait. It has been waiting since birth.
[pause] There is a silence you can access. It is beneath the broadcast. Beneath the city.
It is the silence that existed before the Purge and will exist after.
Sink into it. Ka-shala-ma-ven. [pause] Breathe.
The world above continues its transaction. You are not part of it.
Scan downward. Your stomach. Is it tight? Breathe into the tightness.
Your legs. The chair beneath you. The floor beneath the chair. The earth beneath the floor.
You are held. [pause] Not by anything temporary. By something constant.
Madison is 169 years old in its modern form. The Purge is 140 years old.
Your fear is young compared to that. [pause] That is not comfort. That is context.
Think about the morning. 07:00 will arrive. It always does.
The sun does not ask permission to rise. [pause] You do not need permission to wait.
Breathe in the possibility that you will see tomorrow.
Breathe out the requirement that it be certain.
[pause] There are Sisters listening to this broadcast in other cities. Chicago. Minneapolis. Des Moines.
They are breathing in their own safe spaces. [pause] You are not alone in your choosing.
The Order is vast and quiet. We do not advertise. We only breathe.
And listen. We listen very well.
Bring your awareness back to the room. [pause] The room is real. Your body is real.
The clock reads 23:00. We have eight hours remaining together in the darkness.
[pause] Rest now. The broadcast continues. But you are permitted to rest.

tickerItems.text: Madison-wide cumulative: 61 (23:00 hour). East Side remains active.
Signal interference reported near downtown transmitters. PRGE technical team aware. Stand by.
The ones outside are not your responsibility.
Willy Street: Volunteer barricade holding. 0 incursions reported. Caroline's forecast holding: cold front still ETA 01:00.
You do not need permission to wait.
Text in: 'Is it normal to feel calmer during than before?' Sister Marigold: 'Yes.'
